title: Edge Security, WAF, Hotlinking, Headers, and Failure Containment · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds layered edge-security and abuse-control plan, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of layered edge-security and abuse-control plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
Treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
Set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Waf Rate Bot Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 1433 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to layer application authorization, WAF, origin protection, headers, and safe error caching; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Waf Rate Bot Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply waf, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Waf Rate Bot Boundary, keep Waf Rate Bot Boundary connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Hotlink Origin Secret · changed case · Workshop Hotlink Origin Secret

Build deterministic Go evidence to layer application authorization, WAF, origin protection, headers, and safe error caching; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 7909 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to layer application authorization, WAF, origin protection, headers, and safe error caching; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
